mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals ongoing labor market expansion, which supports aggregate income and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate exceeding one percentage point within two months signals an aggressive monetary easing cycle that compresses the equity discount rate and boosts present values of future cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously declining retail sales and rising unemployment generate a joint demand-labor contraction signal that depresses corporate revenue expectations and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where RSAFS declines and UNRATE rises, positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating CPI inflation forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy, elevating real discount rates and compressing equity valuation multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In tightening cycles where CPIAUCSL accelerates, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production exceeding 2 percent signals acute manufacturing contraction and inventory destocking, which compresses industrial corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,200 thousand units signal robust residential investment and construction employment, which drives durable goods demand and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances where HOUST > 1200, 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth alongside still-positive payroll growth signals a late-cycle soft-landing-to-hard-landing transition, as the labor market lags output. This divergence predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are repriced lower.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for the divergence signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Restrictive monetary policy with FEDFUNDS above 3.0% combined with rising unemployment exceeding 0.2 percentage points in 3 months creates a double drag on aggregate demand and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, positive 60-day returns occur in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year window.
